commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft with broad compatibility, auth, and security-boundary impact needs human maintainer acceptance rather than an automated repair branch.; Cleared: The diff is security-sensitive but appears to harden the boundary; I found no concrete supply-chain or credential-handling regression in the inspected paths. (sha=d78673a9498680c25381e0b0ebc3a0bd19796b9c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Windows node identity, pairing authorization, loopback gateway auth classification, host-native exec approval RPCs, node event routing, Control UI read-only native approval rendering, CLI guidance, and TypeScript build helper loading.

PR surface: Source +1129, Tests +2684, Config 0, Other 0. Total +3813 across 65 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main reproduction was run in this read-only review. The source paths and PR validation make the old Windows node identity, auth classification, and exec-approval routing failures source-reproducible, but maintainers should treat the Windows pond proof as the real behavior evidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft with broad compatibility, auth, and security-boundary impact needs human maintainer acceptance rather than an automated repair branch.

target.title: fix(cli): harden Windows node pairing and approvals
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve stable Windows node/device identity across reconnects and same-key metadata refresh approvals, while still rotating unsafe/stale/revoked/mismatched device tokens
- harden node pairing approval authz, pending/paired node presentation, and gateway node-event routing so Windows nodes are less likely to fall back to manual/TUI repair paths
- route devices/nodes/gateway CLI RPC helpers through direct local gateway-client auth for explicit loopback shared token/password calls, without letting stale inactive env/config tokens suppress device-token fallback
- make host-native Windows exec approval get/set payloads work through the dedicated `exec.approvals.node.*` gateway methods, including native payloads that omit `payloadJSON`
- block raw `node.invoke systemExecApprovals.*` / `system.execApprovals.*` policy mutation paths and keep exec policy mutation on audited dedicated methods
- render host-native Windows node exec approval snapshots read-only in Control UI, including normalization for malformed node-supplied rules
- clarify the `nodes invoke system.run` CLI error so users are pointed to agent `/exec host=node`, not a nonexistent `openclaw exec` command
- make build/package TypeScript helper scripts run through the repo `tsx` loader so Node builds without built-in TypeScript support can complete

## Draft Snapshot
Current pushed draft head: `3d465e67c8685c50cc4263c3d393743bb744e4f3`.

This remains draft for maintainer review, not because of a known validation failure.

## Validation
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm check:changed` passed on Testbox `tbx_01ksz6hgwsfb1jvra3qr31425w`.
- Focused regression passed after latest fixes: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts src/cli/exec-approvals-cli.test.ts src/gateway/server.node-pairing-authz.test.ts src/gateway/node-connect-reconcile.test.ts src/gateway/server.node-invoke-approval-bypass.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/exec-approvals-validators.test.ts` passed 3 shards / 114 tests.
- Targeted `oxlint` passed for the changed CLI/gateway/protocol/node-pairing/UI test files after stale suppression cleanup.
- Final autoreview passed: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- GitHub PR checks at current head show completed success/skipped checks and no attention-required checks at last refresh.

## Windows Node Pond Proof
- Signed prerelease used: `openclaw-windows-node` release `v0.6.0-alpha.14`, asset `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-x64.exe`, digest `sha256:36405bd72e2c11bae0aa051b551b339982a66ffb59820d977ad9983dbf0b4d98`.
- Linux gateway checkout was refreshed to this PR head, rebuilt successfully with `CI=true pnpm build`, and restarted on the active pond gateway port.
- Runtime reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.31 (3d465e6)` and `/health` returned live.
- Pond config drift was remediated by setting `gateway.port` to the active gateway port; default CLI probes now work without manual `--url` / `--token` overrides.
- Pairing proof passed: `nodes list --json` returned 0 pending, 2 paired, 2 connected Windows nodes.
- Exec approval proof passed on both connected Windows nodes: `approvals get --node` returned `enabled=true`, `defaultAction=deny`, `rules=22`, and host-native policy notes.
- Exec boundary proof passed: raw `gateway call node.invoke` for `system.execApprovals.get` is rejected with `node.invoke does not allow system.execApprovals.*; use exec.approvals.node.*`.
- Command proof passed on both nodes: direct `node.invoke system.which` resolved Windows shell binaries, and direct `node.invoke system.run` for `hostname` returned exit code 0.
- Feature smoke passed on both nodes: `device.info`, `device.status`, `camera.list`, and `screen.snapshot` returned successfully; screen snapshots produced non-empty PNG payloads.
- Installed Windows app/signing proof passed on both nodes: running `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.exe` reports product version `0.6.0-alpha.14+Branch.master.Sha.eb06fba21c44b5d2220e83992b8da59bfcc1e67a...`, file version `0.6.0.0`, Authenticode `Valid`, signer `OpenClaw Foundation`, thumbprint `FBBE0F15D46DBD9C99DBEBD1BC4C8EA0311BE4DA`.
- Installer signing proof passed on both nodes: `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-x64.exe` is Authenticode `Valid` with the same OpenClaw signer/thumbprint.

## Release Signing State
- `openclaw-windows-node` CI signs release executables/installers with Azure Trusted Signing endpoint `https://eus.codesigning.azure.net/`, signing account `openclaw`, certificate profile `openclaw`.
- Azure CLI reports Trusted Signing account `openclaw` in resource group `openclawwinnode`, location `eastus`; certificate profile `openclaw` is `Active` / `PublicTrust`.
- GitHub release `v0.6.0-alpha.14` is published as a prerelease and contains x64 setup, arm64 setup, and x64 zip assets with GitHub SHA256 digests.

## Notes
- During pond validation, a previous root-launched gateway had left pairing files owned by root. Ownership was corrected and the gateway was relaunched as the pond user; startup then had no pairing-file permission warnings.
- The stale local gateway on the old port was intentionally left untouched; the active pond proof uses the current PR-head gateway on the configured active port.
target.author: vincentkoc
